set.caption: 2.4 Wave functions for the simple harmonic oscillator — $\psi_{n}(\xi)/\sqrt{\alpha}$ with $\xi=\alpha x$; $\cdots\cdots|\psi_{n}(\xi)|^{2}/\alpha$; the probability distribution for a classical oscillator with energy $E_{n}$. The classical motion is confined to the region between the vertical lines.

block.caption: 2.10 Approximate eigenvalues given by the Rayleigh–Ritz variational method with linear trial functions. Each root $E_{i}^{(N)}$ of the determinantal equation [2.387] is an upper bound on the corresponding exact eigenvalue $E_{i}$ .
